title: Leviticus: Holy Living
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of leviticus: holy living and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study leviticus: holy living and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What repeated command defines the Holiness Code in Leviticus 17-22? Answer: Be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy.
segments.correct_answer: Be holy, for I the LORD your God am holy
acceptable_variants: you shall be holy as I am holy
be holy because god is holy
the command be holy for I am holy appears repeatedly in the holiness code

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: God's fundamental attribute of being set apart from all that is impure or common; Israel is called to reflect this holiness in every area of life
The command in Leviticus 19:18 to love your neighbor as yourself; Jesus identifies this as the second great commandment summarizing the law
